title: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: Macron pays homage to late scholar and Resistance fighter Marc Bloch, who joins Panthéon
Panic sweeps across California over proposed billionaire tax
France's ambiguous memory of slavery
subhead: Although the Code Noir has finally been officially repealed on May 28, it is important to remember that slavery in the French colonies reached its height precisely as the universalist ideals of the Enlightenment were spreading in France, writes journalist Jérôme Gautheret.

title: North Korea is equipping its navy with nuclear weapons, Kim Jong Un says
subhead: The North Korean leader made the announcement on Tuesday, during the commissioning of the 'Choe Hyon,' a 5,000-ton warship.

title: Grieving Deschamps to miss France's match against Norway
subhead: France head coach Didier Deschamps’ mother died on Tuesday, so he will be returning to France for the funeral, with Guy Stéphan taking over for Friday's final Group I game against Norway. The 2026 World Cup is Deschamps' final tournament in charge of France.

title: 'A man in shorts is as competent as a man in a suit': Young people push back against office dress codes
subhead: Some young people push back against traditional dress codes that still prevail in many professional sectors. When temperatures rise, the façade cracks and debates become more heated.

Montréal gunman leaves behind manifesto inspired by the 'incel' movement
subhead: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.

title: Preliminary report on killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna points to serious failings in case handling
subhead: The report, prepared by inspectors from the justice ministry and the national gendarmerie, identified a series of mistakes and a failure to follow through on procedure by the prosecutor's office in Auch and the gendarmerie in Lectoure.
byline: Grégoire Biseau,  Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lucie Soullier

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.
